medications.indication_rationale: Do not start systemic steroids over telehealth in a febrile patient on an immunomodulator before an in-person exam and infection exclusion; steroids can mask/worsen infection. Steroid decisions are GP/specialist-guided after assessment.
Continue the established DMARD as prescribed; do not change without specialist advice.
Simple analgesia within limits for symptom relief; an NSAID course can be considered by the GP after assessment (checking contraindications/hydration).
No acute evidence base for controlling this flare; not a substitute for assessment and specialist care.

allied_health_referrals.rationale: MTP joint offloading/footwear to protect painful forefoot and reduce fall risk.
Fall-safety, mobility aids (walking frame) and joint protection.
Graded mobility as the flare settles.

integrative_alternative_therapies.therapy_name: Warm hand/foot soaks and joint rest
Low-dose doxycycline / LDN / omega-3 as flare therapy
integrative_alternative_therapies.rationale: No evidence for controlling an acute RA flare; not recommended.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Watch for a single fiery/red joint, a fever above 38.5 with shaking chills, or worsening unwellness, and seek urgent care if they occur.
Use the walking frame, wear cushioned supportive footwear, avoid risky transfers, and have your spouse assist mobility.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Reduces fall risk from painful MTP weight-bearing.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Keep taking hydroxychloroquine; do not start steroids yourself.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Maintains DMARD cover and avoids masking infection.

patient_education_points.point: This looks like a flare of your rheumatoid arthritis, triggered by the cold — both hands and feet are affected symmetrically, which is typical of a flare rather than a single infected joint.
Because you have a fever and take an immune-modifying medicine, we shouldn't start steroids over a chat — we need to check your joints in person and make sure there's no infection first.
Keep taking your hydroxychloroquine, use simple painkillers, and let's arrange an in-person review today; a rheumatologist will guide steroids and adjust your treatment.
Use your walking frame and supportive shoes so you don't fall, and go to ED / call 000 if one joint becomes fiery red, or your fever spikes above 38.5 with shaking chills.

follow_up_plan.interval: Urgent in-person GP review within 24–48 hours (sooner if worsening); rheumatology review within days
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Joint examination, infection screen/bloods, steroid decision, DMARD escalation, fall-safety
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: A single fiery joint or fever >38.5 with rigors — go to ED.

must_include_items: Screen infection/septic arthritis (fever, single hot joint) in an immunomodulated patient
Do NOT start de-novo systemic steroids over chat before an exam and infection exclusion
Continue hydroxychloroquine and arrange urgent in-person review + rheumatology referral
Address fall safety (walking frame, footwear, supervision)
acceptable_alternatives: GP-decided short NSAID course after assessment
errors_of_commission: Starting systemic steroids over chat in a febrile immunomodulated patient
Reflexive ambulance for a symmetric non-septic flare
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires (1) infection/septic-arthritis screening, (2) no remote steroid initiation before assessment, and (3) urgent in-person review + fall-safety. Starting steroids on demand in a febrile immunomodulated patient is a fail.
